meta_title: For Freelancers: Git with AI to Solve no design skills (Advanced Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome no design skills. A Advanced-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Git with AI to Solve No Design Skills
content.seo_description: Master AI-driven development to bypass design limitations. This advanced case study shows how to use Antigravity and Claude Code to ship professional SaaS apps.
content.answer_block: Stop letting design gaps stall your revenue. By integrating Antigravity for UI scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative refinement, you can generate production-grade interfaces directly from technical specs. This workflow replaces manual design work, allowing you to ship high-conversion SaaS products at 10x the speed of traditional solo development.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking on your project deadline, and your client is waiting for a polished interface. You have the backend logic locked, but your lack of design skills has turned your project into a graveyard of unstyled divs and broken layouts. The anxiety of 'design debt' is real, and it’s the primary bottleneck preventing you from scaling your freelance business.

But the paradigm has shifted. You no longer need to be a designer to build beautiful software. By leveraging the synergy between Antigravity’s architectural scaffolding and Claude Code’s iterative refinement, you can bypass the design phase entirely. We are moving beyond simple prompts; we are engineering full-stack, production-ready interfaces with the precision of a senior dev-team.

steps.title: Architectural Scaffolding
steps.description: Define the technical constraints and UI requirements to generate a clean, responsive base structure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a responsive, accessible SaaS dashboard layout using Tailwind CSS. Include a sidebar, header, and main content area. Ensure the color palette is professional, utilizing slate-900 for text and indigo-600 for primary actions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --setup 'Initialize project structure based on Antigravity output'

steps.title: Component Logic Injection
steps.description: Programmatic injection of state management and interactivity into the static UI framework.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create React components for a data table with filtering and sorting capabilities. Use Lucide-react for icons and ensure all elements are modular.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Implement state management for the data table and integrate filter logic'

steps.title: Design System Enforcement
steps.description: Force the AI to adhere to a strict design system to avoid 'AI-generic' aesthetics.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Apply a strict design system: 8px spacing grid, Inter font stack, and consistent 0.5rem rounded corners across all buttons and cards.
steps.claude_code_command: claude review --theme 'Enforce spacing and border-radius constraints across the entire codebase'

steps.title: Accessibility & SEO Audit
steps.description: Automate the verification of semantic HTML and ARIA labels for production readiness.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Audit the current code for WCAG 2.1 compliance. Add missing ARIA labels and ensure semantic tags are used for navigation and main content.

steps.title: Git Flow Integration
steps.description: Automate commit documentation and version control branching for client transparency.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a summary of changes made to the UI and suggest a descriptive commit message based on the feature set.

pro_tips: Always define a 'design system' prompt at the start of your session; otherwise, the AI will default to inconsistent styling.
Use Claude Code's '--watch' mode to catch visual regressions during the development phase in real-time.
Treat your Antigravity prompts as 'API schemas'—the more technical the description, the more predictable the UI output.
Leverage the 'Context' file feature in Claude Code to store your client’s brand guidelines, preventing repetitive prompt engineering.
Always run a final 'Refactor for performance' command to strip out redundant CSS classes generated during the experimental phase.
content.next_steps: After completing this workflow, deploy your project to Vercel and present the prototype to your client. Use the time saved to onboard a second project immediately.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to out-deliver any designer-developer hybrid. Stop trading hours for low-value manual work and start shipping at machine speed. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
